mission: IPC and event handler runtime issue hunt

package_roles.opencode: Backend server (forked as sidecar utility process)

ipc_architecture: 4-channel typed IPC with compile-time contract enforcement

ipc_channel_gaps.detail: Orphaned channel definition at ipc-channels.ts:73, typed in contract at ipc-contract.ts:244, but has NO ipcMain.handle() handler. If a future developer wires it to the preload bridge, Electron will throw 'No handler registered' at runtime. Likely a leftover from planned feature.
ipcMain.handle() registered at ipc-capabilities.ts:25, but NOT exposed in preload window.api. No renderer code calls it. The handler exists but is unreachable from any renderer.

critical.issue: Calls window.api.getGitStatus() — doesn't exist in ElectronAPI type. Uses unsafe cast `window as unknown as { api?: Record<string, unknown> }` to bypass TS. In Electron, typeof check fails, falls through to HTTP /api/git/status. Git watcher silently provides no status in production.
Same pattern — calls window.api.getDependencyStatus() through unsafe cast. Falls through to /api/deps/status.
Same pattern — calls window.api.getSessionMemory(). Falls through to /api/session/memory.
Same pattern — calls window.api.getTestStatus(). Falls through to /api/tests/status.
Same pattern — calls window.api.getPullRequestStatus(). Falls through to /api/pr/status.

notable.issue: safeInvoke imported from ipc-contract but NEVER called. Dead code that contributes to IpcResult<IpcResult<T>> risk if ever used.

ipc-contract.ts defines a DerivedElectronAPI (line 443) meant to replace the manual ElectronAPI, but preload/index.ts:2 still imports the manual type from preload/types.ts. The manual type can drift from the actual IPC contract over time.

BrowserApi polyfill and ElectronAPI have diverged. BrowserApi has: getGitStatus, getDependencyStatus, getSessionMemory, getTestStatus, getPullRequestStatus, openDirectoryPickerDialog, navigateToRoute — NOT in ElectronAPI. ElectronAPI has: pluginSend, pluginOn, pluginOff, pluginInvoke, sessionExportData, sessionImportFile — NOT in BrowserApi.

listener_leaks.name: GlobalBus singleton EventEmitter
listener_leaks.detail: Module-level singleton. Production code consistently cleans up with GlobalBus.off() or Effect.addFinalizer(). Verified in: control-plane/util.ts (off), server/httpapi/handlers/global.ts (off in finalizer), project/project.ts, sync/index.ts. No leak risk in production path.

listener_leaks.name: Preload IPC push listeners
listener_leaks.detail: All 7 ipcRenderer.on() listeners have cleanup: awaitInitialization uses .finally() → removeListener, on* methods return cleanup functions, pluginOn/pluginOff use _pluginListeners Map + removeListener.

listener_leaks.name: Plugin transport handler Maps (main process)
listener_leaks.detail: Module-level Maps with unsubscribe functions that delete from Sets and clean up empty channels. Good pattern.

listener_leaks.name: Plugin listener key collision risk
listener_leaks.detail: pluginOn() uses `const key = ${channel}::${String(handler)}` — String(handler) on a function returns source code text. Two anonymous functions with identical bodies produce the same key, causing overwrite. Risk is low since plugin handlers are typically unique named functions.

listener_leaks.name: WebSocket handler cleanup
listener_leaks.detail: connectResponsesWebSocket and streamResponsesWebSocket both properly call socket.off() on all handlers in their cleanup functions. WS pool invalidate at ws-pool.ts:247 adds a no-op error handler but immediately terminates the socket — harmless.

solid_effect_issues.details: All createEffect calls read reactive values synchronously with no subscriptions needing cleanup. onMount uses makeEventListener (auto-cleanup). onCleanup used consistently for cleanup of animation frames, scroll refs, resize handlers, event listeners. The @solid-primitives/event-listener library auto-detaches on component unmount.

websocket_handler_issues.details: connectResponsesWebSocket and streamResponsesWebSocket properly clean up all socket listeners (message, error, close) via .off() in cleanup/onAbort callbacks. OAuth callback at mcp/oauth-callback.ts needs review but is a short-lived connection.

payload_concerns.payload: Returns string (ZIP archive, up to 50 MB configured at logging.ts:49)
payload_concerns.detail: Returns Promise<string> containing up to 50 MB of log data through IPC as a single string. Could cause renderer frame drops or OOM.

payload_concerns.payload: ArrayBuffer (image pixel data, can be multi-MB)
payload_concerns.detail: ArrayBuffer must be cloned by Electron's structured clone algorithm. Large clipboard images cause serialization overhead.

payload_concerns.payload: Full session data as string (potentially hundreds of KB)
payload_concerns.detail: Entire session data passes through IPC as a string. readFileSync/writeFileSync in main process blocks the event loop.
Large JSON responses from GitHub API (e.g., file listings) pass through IPC in their entirety.

type_safety_gaps.detail: ElectronAPI.pluginOn at preload/types.ts:176 has handler type `(data: unknown) => void` but PluginTransportHandler at preload/types.ts:77 has `(channel: string, data: unknown) => void`. The `channel` parameter is missing from the ElectronAPI type. When plugin-transport.ts:78 calls api.pluginOn(prefix + channel, handler), the handler type mismatch is invisible because the preload wraps it.

type_safety_gaps.gap: Config handlers use unknown[] with runtime validation
type_safety_gaps.detail: SET_DESKTOP_CUSTOM_AGENTS, SET_DESKTOP_MCP_SERVERS, SET_DESKTOP_PLUGIN_CONFIG accept unknown[] — type safety deferred to runtime validators.
store:set accepts value: unknown — no compile-time enforcement of stored values.

naming_inconsistencies.detail: 3 plugin channels use `opencode:plugin:invoke/send/push` with double-colon sub-namespace, while all other channels use flat `opencode:channel-name`. EVENT_EXPLAIN also uses double-colon: `opencode:event:explain`.

withIpcResult() receives dot-notation names (e.g., 'window.count', 'store.get', 'fs.openDirectoryPicker') while IPC channels use kebab-case (e.g., 'opencode:get-window-count'). Two different naming systems.

critical_issues: CRITICAL: 5 watcher phantom API calls — packages/app/src/ambient/watchers/git-status.ts, deps.ts, session-memory.ts, test-status.ts, pr-activity.ts all call methods on window.api (getGitStatus, getDependencyStatus, getSessionMemory, getTestStatus, getPullRequestStatus) that do NOT exist in the ElectronAPI type or preload bridge. All use unsafe `window as unknown` cast to bypass TypeScript. In Electron production, these silently fail and fall through to HTTP endpoints that may not exist.
MODERATE: Orphaned EVENT_EXPLAIN channel — defined in channels + contract but has NO ipcMain.handle handler. Will fail with 'No handler registered' if wired to preload.
MODERATE: GET_CAPABILITIES handler — registered with ipcMain.handle but NOT exposed in preload window.api. The handler exists but is unreachable.

MODERATE: Plugin transport handler type mismatch — ElectronAPI.pluginOn handler type `(data) => void` differs from PluginTransportHandler `(channel, data) => void`. The `channel` parameter is lost in the type definition.
MINOR: DerivedElectronAPI unused — contract defines auto-derived API type but preload uses manually-written ElectronAPI. Potential drift.
MINOR: safeInvoke dead import — imported in preload/index.ts but never called.
MINOR: Plugin listener key collision — String(handler) as Map key risks collisions for anonymous functions with identical bodies.

overall_assessment.ipc_architecture: WELL-STRUCTURED — typed constants, contract enforcement, centralized registration, 4-channel pattern
overall_assessment.listener_cleanup: GOOD — consistent cleanup across preload, GlobalBus, WebSockets, plugin transport
overall_assessment.solidjs_effects: CLEAN — no lifecycle issues found (makeEventListener auto-cleanup, onCleanup used for all subscriptions)
main_risks: 5 phantom API calls in watchers that silently fail in Electron production
1 unreachable handler (GET_CAPABILITIES) not exposed in preload
recommendations: Add missing watcher methods (getGitStatus, getDependencyStatus, getSessionMemory, getTestStatus, getPullRequestStatus) to either the Electron preload bridge or remove the brute-force cast pattern and use direct HTTP calls only
Either wire EVENT_EXPLAIN to an actual handler or remove from channel registry + contract
Expose GET_CAPABILITIES in the preload bridge or remove the registered handler
Fix pluginOn handler type signature to include channel parameter
Replace manual ElectronAPI with DerivedElectronAPI from contract to prevent drift
